introSection: Magic Flyer was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by US manufacturer Bradley & Kaye formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
From 1946 until 1970, this roller coaster operated at Beverly Park in Los Angeles, California, USA.

In 1970, the coaster was sold to Magic Mountain, and opened at the beginning of the park's inaugural season in May 1971, where it was known as Clown Coaster. The ride featured two cars, colored red with orange stripes. For the 1985 season, with the rebranding of Children's World into Bugs Bunny World, the coaster was renamed Wile E. Coyote Coaster.

After the 1998 season, the coaster was removed from the park and was stored. It was later rebuilt and reopened under the name of Goliath Jr., to commemorate the the park's then-new hyper coaster in 2001. The color of the cars became dark blue and the ride's support structure was heavily modified in-house to resemble Goliath's support structure. The ride was painted into Goliath's colors, oranage track and blue supports.

In 2007, Six Flags signed a license agreement with HIT Entertainment to open up themed areas based on Thomas & Friends titled "Thomas Town". The portion where Goliath Jr. sat would become part of the area, and the ride was given an extensive refurbishment to fit the new theme. The coaster, now named Percy's Railway, opened with the rest of Thomas Town on June 6, 2008 in time for the park's summer season. The front car was given an enclosed Percy-shaped interior, while the other two cars became Troublesome Trucks.

In October 2010, after exiting bankruptcy, Six Flags announced that they would remove all licensed theming from their parks except for their Warner Bros. license. In 2011, Thomas Town was rebranded as "Whistlestop Park", with the coaster gaining its current name at the start of the 2011 season. Very little change was made to the coaster aside from Percy's face being removed (replaced with a generic train front) alongside the removal of the engine's stripes.

On December 8, 2025, a permit was filed by the park for the demolition of the roller coaster. On December 16, it was announced that the coaster would close to make way for the new Looney Tunes Land, which will replace Whistlestop Park and Bugs Bunny World. The coaster alongside Tweety's Escape will be replaced with a new children's play area entitled Taz's Exploration Trail.

On December 30, it was announced that the coaster would officially close on January 5, 2026. However, the ride may have operated later in January, along with Tweety's Escape.

The ride was being disassembled by January 12, 2026.

sections.title: Magic Flyer
sections.content: Light brown track and supports. This color scheme was also used on Percy's Railway.

sections.title: Clown Coaster
sections.content: Unknown track color and light brown supports.

fullText:
Magic Flyer was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by US manufacturer Bradley & Kaye formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain in Valencia, California, USA.

From 1946 until 1970, this roller coaster operated at Beverly Park in Los Angeles, California, USA.

In 1970, the coaster was sold to Magic Mountain, and opened at the beginning of the park's inaugural season in May 1971, where it was known as Clown Coaster. The ride featured two cars, colored red with orange stripes. For the 1985 season, with the rebranding of Children's World into Bugs Bunny World, the coaster was renamed Wile E. Coyote Coaster.

After the 1998 season, the coaster was removed from the park and was stored. It was later rebuilt and reopened under the name of Goliath Jr., to commemorate the the park's then-new hyper coaster in 2001. The color of the cars became dark blue and the ride's support structure was heavily modified in-house to resemble Goliath's support structure. The ride was painted into Goliath's colors, oranage track and blue supports.

In 2007, Six Flags signed a license agreement with HIT Entertainment to open up themed areas based on Thomas & Friends titled "Thomas Town". The portion where Goliath Jr. sat would become part of the area, and the ride was given an extensive refurbishment to fit the new theme. The coaster, now named Percy's Railway, opened with the rest of Thomas Town on June 6, 2008 in time for the park's summer season. The front car was given an enclosed Percy-shaped interior, while the other two cars became Troublesome Trucks.

In October 2010, after exiting bankruptcy, Six Flags announced that they would remove all licensed theming from their parks except for their Warner Bros. license. In 2011, Thomas Town was rebranded as "Whistlestop Park", with the coaster gaining its current name at the start of the 2011 season. Very little change was made to the coaster aside from Percy's face being removed (replaced with a generic train front) alongside the removal of the engine's stripes.

On December 8, 2025, a permit was filed by the park for the demolition of the roller coaster. On December 16, it was announced that the coaster would close to make way for the new Looney Tunes Land, which will replace Whistlestop Park and Bugs Bunny World. The coaster alongside Tweety's Escape will be replaced with a new children's play area entitled Taz's Exploration Trail.

On December 30, it was announced that the coaster would officially close on January 5, 2026. However, the ride may have operated later in January, along with Tweety's Escape.

The ride was being disassembled by January 12, 2026.
